scenario: Lyric Marketplace operates an order-fulfillment process in Camunda 8.8 that fans out to two concurrent tasks B and C handling roughly 9000 orders per day. When task C completes a terminate end event is reached at the process level, and the team needs to predict precisely what happens to the still-active token at task B in the same flow scope.
question: What does the engine do when the process instance reaches the terminate end event at the process level?

options.text: The engine terminates all element instances in the same flow scope as the end event, including the active task B, and the process instance completes without taking outgoing flows
The engine completes the token at task C and leaves task B active until it completes naturally, after which the process instance finally ends and reports completion
The engine compensates the already-completed task C and waits for task B to complete before considering the process instance terminated by the end event
The engine terminates only the token that reached the end event and lets task B continue independently as a detached element instance until a separate cancel is issued

a.text: Correct. When a process instance reaches a terminate end event, the engine terminates all element instances in the same flow scope as the end event, so any concurrent token such as the one on task B is cancelled along with the rest of that scope.

b.text: Incorrect. The terminate end event does not wait for concurrent activities to finish naturally; it actively cancels the other element instances in the same flow scope on arrival.

c.text: Incorrect. The terminate end event terminates element instances in the flow scope; it does not trigger compensation of already-completed activities such as task C.

d.text: Incorrect. A terminate end event affects the whole flow scope rather than just the arriving token, so concurrent activities like task B are cancelled rather than left running as detached instances.

explanation: A terminate end event is the only kind of terminate event. When a process instance reaches it, all element instances in the same flow scope are terminated, which is the documented way to cancel concurrent flows that are no longer required.
